examples.english: I stayed at home all weekend.
examples.chinese: 我整個週末都待在家裡。

home.english: the place where you were born or feel you belong

examples.english: Taipei will always be my home.

home.english: to or at the place where you live
connected with the home or family

field.english: an area of land used for crops or animals

examples.english: The cows are in the field.

field.english: an area of study, work, or activity

examples.chinese: 醫學是一個艱深的領域。

field.english: an area where a sport is played

examples.english: The players ran onto the field.

field.english: to send out a team, candidate, or representative

examples.english: The party will field two candidates.
examples.chinese: 該黨會派出兩名候選人。

stand.english: to be upright on your feet
to accept or bear something unpleasant

examples.english: She took a clear stand on the issue.
examples.chinese: 她對這個議題表明了清楚的立場。

stand.english: a thing used to support or display something

examples.english: Put the phone on the stand.
He stayed true to his family.

voice.english: the sound produced when a person speaks or sings
the right or ability to express an opinion

examples.english: Students should have a voice in the decision.
examples.chinese: 學生應該在決定中有發言權。

voice.english: to express a feeling or opinion in words

cut.english: to divide or damage something with a sharp tool

examples.english: She cut the bread with a knife.

cut.english: to reduce the amount or size of something
an injury or opening made by something sharp

examples.english: He had a small cut on his hand.
examples.chinese: 他的手上有一個小傷口。

cut.english: a reduction in amount or size

fast.english: to eat no food for a period of time

examples.english: Some people fast for health reasons.
examples.chinese: 有些人為了健康而禁食。

fast.english: a period when you do not eat food

examples.english: He broke his fast at sunset.
examples.chinese: 他在日落時結束禁食期。

fight.english: to use physical force against someone
to try hard to stop or achieve something

examples.chinese: 他們為平等權利而抗爭。

examples.english: A fight broke out outside the bar.
examples.chinese: 酒吧外發生了一場打架。

love.english: a strong feeling of deep affection
to feel deep affection for someone or like something very much

open.english: not closed or available to use
to move something so it is no longer closed
an open or public place or situation

examples.english: They discussed the plan in the open.

side.english: one of the surfaces or parts away from the middle

examples.english: Write your name on the side of the box.
examples.chinese: 把你的名字寫在盒子的側面。

side.english: one of two or more groups in a dispute or game
at or on the side; additional

act.english: to perform a role in a play or film

examples.english: Helping him was a kind act.
examples.chinese: 幫助他是一個善良的行為。

act.english: a law passed by a government

bit.english: a small amount or piece of something

examples.english: There were bits of glass on the floor.

bit.english: the smallest unit of computer information

examples.chinese: 一個位元組有八個位元。

great.english: large in amount, size, or degree

examples.english: There was great interest in the project.
examples.chinese: 大家對這個專案有很大的興趣。
我們度過了很好的時光。

examples.english: The cup fell to the ground.

ground.english: a reason or basis for something

examples.english: There are no grounds for complaint.

ground.english: to stop a plane from flying or a child from going out

examples.chinese: 暴風雨使所有航班停飛。
我們看了一個喜劇節目。

amount.english: a quantity or sum of something

examples.english: A large amount of money was missing.

amount.english: to add up to or be equal to

bear.english: to accept or endure something difficult

examples.english: She could not bear the pain.

bear.english: to give birth to or produce

examples.english: The tree bears fruit in summer.
examples.chinese: 這棵樹在夏天結出果實。

bear.english: a large strong wild animal with thick fur

examples.english: It took a great deal of time.

deal.english: to take action about a problem or situation

examples.english: We must deal with this problem.
examples.chinese: 我們必須處理這個問題。

hard.english: firm and difficult to bend or break
with a lot of effort or force

examples.chinese: 每位家長都收到一封信。

examples.english: The parent company is in Japan.

state.english: the condition that someone or something is in

examples.english: The room was in a terrible state.

state.english: a political unit within a country
to say or write something clearly

turn.english: to move around a point or change direction
to become or make something become different

examples.english: The leaves turn yellow in autumn.

turn.english: a chance or time to do something

examples.english: It is your turn to speak.

catch.english: to stop and hold something moving
to be in time for a bus, train, or event

examples.chinese: 我們趕上了最後一班火車。

catch.english: something caught or the act of catching

check.english: to examine something to make sure it is correct or safe

examples.english: Do a quick check before you leave.
examples.chinese: 離開前做一次快速檢查。

check.english: a restaurant bill or a written order to pay money

examples.english: Can we have the check, please?

clear.english: without clouds, dirt, or objects blocking the view
to remove things from a place

front.english: the part of something that faces forward
to face a particular direction or place

join.english: to become a member of a group
to put or bring things together

examples.english: Join the two pieces of wood.

meet.english: to see and speak to someone
to satisfy a need or condition

examples.chinese: 這個計畫滿足我們的需要。

examples.english: That was real nice of you.
Write your name at the top.

top.english: highest in position, rank, or quality
to be higher or better than something

wrong.english: not suitable or not working properly

examples.english: Something is wrong with my phone.

examples.chinese: 你把我的名字拼錯誤地。

age.english: the number of years someone has lived or something has existed

examples.english: We live in the digital age.

age.english: to become older or make something look older

examples.chinese: 這張照片老化得很嚴重。

away.english: to or at a different place
at a distance in space or time

examples.english: The station is two miles away.

complete.english: to finish doing or making something

force.english: an organized group, especially military or police
to make someone do something against their will

form.english: a document with spaces for information
the shape, type, or structure of something

examples.english: Water can take the form of ice.

hold.english: to keep something in your hand or arms
to organize an event or meeting

examples.english: They will hold a meeting tomorrow.

hold.english: the act or way of holding something

examples.english: Keep a firm hold on the rope.

job.english: paid work or a position of employment
a task or piece of work

examples.english: Cleaning the garage is a big job.
examples.chinese: 清理車庫是一項大任務。

kind.english: a type of person or thing

examples.english: What kind of music do you like?
examples.chinese: 你喜歡哪一種類的音樂？

examples.english: She is very kind to children.

land.english: the solid surface of the earth, not sea

examples.english: We could see land from the boat.

land.english: ground used for farming, building, or ownership

examples.english: They bought a piece of land.

land.english: to come down to the ground or make a plane come down

late.english: arriving or happening after the expected time

examples.english: Her late husband was a teacher.
examples.chinese: 她已故的丈夫是一名教師。

late.english: after the expected or usual time

examples.english: There is a mark on the wall.

mark.english: a score given for schoolwork or an exam
to put a sign on something

examples.english: Mark the date on your calendar.
examples.chinese: 在日曆上標記這個日期。

sound.english: to seem a particular way when heard or described
in good condition or based on good judgement

try.english: to make an effort to do something
to use or test something to see if it is good

examples.chinese: 我可以試穿這件外套嗎？

able.english: having the power or skill to do something
skilful or good at doing things

care.english: the process of looking after someone or something
serious attention to avoid harm or mistakes
to feel that something is important

examples.english: I am certain he will come.

certain.english: particular but not named or described exactly

cover.english: to put something over or on top of something

examples.english: Cover the pot with a lid.

cover.english: to include or deal with a subject
the outside front of a book or something that protects an object

examples.chinese: 這本書的封面是藍色的。

develop.english: to grow, change, or make something more advanced
to create a new product, idea, or system

examples.chinese: 他們研發了一個新應用程式。

during.english: throughout or at some time in a period

feel.english: to experience an emotion or physical sensation
to touch something or have a particular texture

examples.english: Feel how soft this cloth is.
examples.chinese: 觸摸看看這塊布多柔軟。

feel.english: the quality or impression something gives

examples.english: This room has a warm feel.
examples.chinese: 這個房間有溫暖的感覺。

kid.english: to joke or trick someone playfully

later.english: at a time in the future

mind.english: the part of a person that thinks and feels
to be annoyed or worried by something
to take care of or pay attention to something

once.english: one time or at some time in the past

examples.english: The start of the film was slow.
She gave a talk on health.
examples.chinese: 她做了一場關於健康的演講。

watch.english: to look at someone or something for a period of time
to pay attention to something carefully
a small clock worn on the wrist
the act of watching for danger or change

examples.english: The guard kept watch all night.

as.english: used in comparisons to mean to the same degree

examples.english: She is as tall as her brother.

as.english: in the role or function of

examples.english: As I was leaving, the phone rang.
As it was late, we went home.
examples.chinese: 因為時間晚了，我們回家了。

by.english: not later than a particular time
used to show the person or thing that does an action

examples.english: The book was written by a doctor.
examples.chinese: 這本書由一位醫生寫成。

one.english: a person, especially people in general

step.english: a movement made by lifting and putting down a foot

examples.english: The first step is to save the file.
examples.chinese: 第一個步驟是儲存檔案。

step.english: to move by lifting and putting down a foot

examples.english: Do not step on the grass.

thick.english: having a large distance between opposite sides
growing close together or difficult to see through

examples.english: The forest was thick and dark.

bite.english: to use the teeth to cut into something

examples.chinese: 那隻狗可能會咬陌生人。

bite.english: an injury made by teeth, or a small amount of food cut by teeth

examples.english: He took a bite of the apple.

get.english: to receive, obtain, or buy something

examples.english: I need to get a new phone.
examples.chinese: 我需要得到一部新手機。

examples.english: What time did you get home?

of.english: used to show belonging or connection

examples.english: This is the cover of the book.

of.english: used to show what something is made from or contains

examples.english: The cup is made of glass.

on.english: touching and supported by a surface

examples.english: The keys are on the table.

west.english: the direction where the sun sets, or the western part of an area

examples.english: The sun sets in the west.
They live on the west coast.

cloud.english: a white or grey mass of very small drops of water in the sky

examples.chinese: 憤怒會使你的判斷模糊。

down.english: towards or in a lower position
from a higher to a lower point along something

examples.english: He felt down after the exam.

dry.english: to remove water from something or become free of water

examples.english: Dry your hands with this towel.
examples.chinese: 用這條毛巾使雙手變乾。

fruit.english: the sweet part of a plant that contains seeds and can be eaten

gate.english: a movable barrier used to close an opening in a fence or wall

examples.chinese: 請把你身後的大門關上。

net.english: material made of strings tied together with spaces between them

examples.english: The fish were caught in a net.

net.english: remaining after costs or taxes have been taken away

north.english: the direction to your left when facing the rising sun, or the northern part of an area

examples.english: The town lies to the north.

package.english: an object wrapped for sending, or a set of related things sold together

examples.chinese: 今天早上有一個包裹送到。

package.english: to put something in a box or wrapping

examples.english: The cookies are packaged in small bags.

tail.english: the part that sticks out at the back of an animal’s body

examples.english: She is a woman with great courage.
examples.chinese: 她是一位具有勇氣的女性。

acid.english: a chemical substance that can react with metals and has a sour taste
containing acid or having the qualities of an acid

at.english: used to show a place, point, or time

examples.english: Meet me at the station at six.

bubble.english: a ball of air or gas in liquid or a thin film

examples.english: Put a dot at the end of the line.

dot.english: to put small marks or objects in many places

double.english: twice as much or as many
to become or make something twice as large

dress.english: a piece of clothing for women or girls that covers the body and part of the legs

examples.chinese: 她穿了一條紅色連身裙。

dress.english: to put clothes on yourself or someone else

flesh.english: the soft part of a body between skin and bone

examples.english: The thorn went into his flesh.

get out.english: to leave or escape from a place

examples.english: We need to get out of the building.
examples.chinese: 我們需要離開這棟大樓。

no.english: used to give a negative answer

node.english: a point where lines or systems meet, especially in a network

examples.english: Each computer is a node on the network.
examples.chinese: 每部電腦都是網絡上的節點。

examples.english: The location is a big plus.

rank.english: a position in an organization or scale

examples.english: He rose to the rank of captain.

rank.english: to have or give a position in an order

examples.english: The school ranks first in the city.
examples.chinese: 這所學校在全市排名第一。

roar.english: to make a very loud deep sound

examples.english: We heard the roar of the engine.
examples.chinese: 我們聽到引擎的轟鳴聲。

shell.english: the hard outer covering of an egg, nut, or animal
an explosive projectile fired from a large gun

sweet.english: a small piece of sweet food

track.english: a path, road, or line used for travel or racing
to follow the movement or progress of someone or something

examples.english: The app can track your steps.
examples.chinese: 這個應用程式可以追蹤你的步數。

examples.english: The cat is under the table.

under.english: less than a particular amount or level

examples.chinese: 五歲以下兒童免費入場。

examples.english: A lamp hung above the table.

above.english: earlier in a piece of writing

all.english: the whole amount or every one of a group

examples.chinese: 所有的學生都必須出席。

all right.english: used to agree or to show acceptance

band.english: a group of musicians who play together
a thin flat strip used for fastening or holding things

examples.english: Tie it with a rubber band.

between.english: in the space separating two people or things

examples.english: The bank is between the school and the shop.
examples.chinese: 銀行在學校和商店之間。

branch.english: a part of a tree growing from the trunk, or a local office of a business

examples.english: The bank opened a new branch.
The road branches after the bridge.

connect.english: to join two or more things together

examples.english: Connect the cable to the computer.

cool.english: slightly cold in a pleasant way
to become less hot or make something less hot

examples.english: Let the soup cool before eating.

degree.english: the amount or level of something

examples.english: The degree of risk is low.

degree.english: a qualification given by a university

examples.english: She has a degree in law.

fire.english: flames and heat, or an uncontrolled burning
to dismiss someone from a job
to shoot a gun or weapon

examples.english: The soldiers fired at the target.
The lake was covered with ice.

into.english: to the inside of a place or thing
used to show a change of state

lead.english: to guide or be in charge of someone or something

leaf.english: a flat green part of a plant

examples.english: A leaf fell from the tree.
The truck carried a heavy load.

load.english: to put things into a vehicle or container

examples.english: They loaded boxes onto the truck.
examples.chinese: 他們把箱子裝載到卡車上。

examples.english: Stand in the middle of the room.

paint.english: a coloured liquid put on a surface
to cover a surface with paint or make a picture with paint

examples.english: The ring is made of silver.
I could not find a single mistake.

single.english: a song released separately from an album

examples.english: The singer released a new single.
examples.chinese: 那位歌手發行了一首新單曲。

examples.english: It was raining, so we stayed home.
examples.chinese: 下雨了，所以我們留在家裏。

tower.english: a tall narrow building or structure

transfer.english: to move from one place, job, or vehicle to another

examples.english: Transfer the files to your laptop.
examples.chinese: 把檔案轉移到你的筆記型電腦。

transfer.english: the act of moving from one place or vehicle to another

advance.english: to move forward or make progress
progress or money paid before it is due

examples.chinese: 醫學進步拯救了許多生命。

against.english: opposing or disagreeing with someone or something

examples.english: Many people voted against the plan.
examples.chinese: 許多人投票反對這個計劃。

examples.english: The course is for children aged five to seven.
examples.chinese: 這課程適合五至七歲的兒童。

examples.english: She cared for her aged parents.

ancient.english: belonging to a very distant past

angle.english: the space between two lines or surfaces that meet
to place something so that it points in a particular direction

examples.english: Angle the lamp toward the desk.

before.english: earlier than a time or event

examples.chinese: 你離開以前打電話給我。

behind.english: at or to the back of someone or something

examples.english: The garden is behind the house.

below.english: later in a piece of writing

bill.english: a document showing how much money is owed

examples.english: Can we have the bill, please?

bond.english: a close connection, or a certificate of debt

examples.english: There is a strong bond between them.
examples.chinese: 他們之間有很強的聯繫。

examples.english: The team bonded during the trip.
examples.chinese: 團隊在旅途中建立關係。

burn.english: to be on fire, or to damage with fire or heat

examples.english: Be careful not to burn your hand.

burn.english: an injury caused by heat or fire

examples.chinese: 在緊急情況下保持平靜。

calm.english: to make someone or something peaceful

can.english: to be able to do something
a metal container for food or drink

cent.english: a unit of money equal to one hundredth of a dollar or euro

circle.english: a round shape with every point the same distance from the centre

examples.english: Draw a circle on the paper.

collect.english: to bring things together or gather them over time

column.english: a vertical section of text or numbers, or a tall support

examples.english: Write the total in the last column.

conduct.english: to organize and carry out an activity, or direct musicians

constant.english: happening all the time or repeatedly

examples.english: The constant noise made sleep difficult.
examples.chinese: 持續的噪音令人難以入睡。

constant.english: a number or quantity that does not change

examples.english: In this formula, k is a constant.
examples.chinese: 在這個公式中，k 是常數。
她吃了一塊朱古力餅乾。

cry.english: to produce tears, or to shout loudly

examples.english: We heard a cry for help.

deliver.english: to take goods or messages somewhere, or give a speech

examples.chinese: 他們每天晚上遞送食物。

divide.english: to separate into parts, or calculate how many times one number contains another

examples.english: Divide the cake into six pieces.

enough.english: as much or as many as needed

feed.english: to give food to a person or animal, or supply something

flight.english: a journey by air, or the act of flying

frame.english: a border or structure that supports something

examples.english: The photo is in a wooden frame.

frame.english: to put a frame around something or express something in a particular way

freeze.english: to become ice or make something very cold and solid

examples.english: Water freezes at zero degrees Celsius.

grass.english: a common green plant with narrow leaves, or an area covered by it

examples.english: Do not walk on the grass.
Heat the oil in a pan.

heel.english: the back part of the foot or shoe

hole.english: an empty space in a surface or object

examples.english: There is a hole in my sock.

hollow.english: a low or empty area in a surface

image.english: a picture, or the idea people have of someone or something

examples.english: The screen showed a clear image.

knife.english: a tool with a sharp blade used for cutting

examples.english: Cut the bread with a knife.

loose.english: not firmly fixed or not tight

metal.english: a hard shiny material such as iron, gold, or silver

examples.english: The door is made of metal.

needle.english: a small thin pointed tool used for sewing or medical injections

pair.english: two things used together or two people connected in some way

examples.english: I bought a pair of shoes.

path.english: a way or track for walking, or a route to a goal

examples.english: Follow the path through the forest.

pig.english: a farm animal with short legs and a flat nose

examples.english: The pig rolled in the mud.

pipe.english: a tube that carries liquid or gas, or a tube used for smoking

quiet.english: to make someone or something quiet

return.english: to go or come back, or give something back
the act of going back or giving something back

examples.chinese: 她的返回令所有人驚訝。

salt.english: a white substance used to flavour food

sand.english: very small grains of rock found on beaches and deserts

examples.english: The children played in the sand.

size.english: how large or small something is

examples.english: What size shoes do you wear?

slow.english: to become slower or make something slower

examples.english: The car slowed near the school.

solid.english: firm and hard, or not liquid or gas
a substance that is not a liquid or gas

some.english: an amount or number of something, not stated exactly
an amount or number of people or things

source.english: the place, person, or thing something comes from

examples.english: What is the source of this information?
examples.chinese: 這些資料的來源是甚麼？

stick.english: to become fixed to something, or push something into something

examples.english: The label will stick to the box.

surface.english: the outside or top layer of something

examples.english: Clean the surface of the table.

tender.english: gentle and kind, or easy to cut or chew

ticket.english: a printed or electronic document giving permission to travel or enter, or a notice of a fine

tire.english: to become tired or make someone tired
a thick rubber ring around a wheel

examples.english: The car needs a new tire.
examples.chinese: 這輛車需要一個新輪胎。

tool.english: an object used to do a job

examples.english: A hammer is a useful tool.

total.english: to add up to an amount

tube.english: a long hollow object, usually round

examples.english: The cream comes in a tube.
She felt weak after the illness.

yellow.english: having the colour of lemons or ripe bananas
the colour of lemons or ripe bananas

examples.chinese: 黃色是我最喜歡的顏色。
我們談了關於這個計劃的事。

after.english: later than a time or event

examples.chinese: 你到達以後打電話給我。

aid.english: help, especially in a difficult situation

examples.english: The money will aid poor families.
examples.chinese: 這筆錢會援助貧困家庭。

back.english: the part of the body opposite the chest, or the rear part of something
to or in a previous place or condition
to support a person or plan

bark.english: the outer covering of a tree

examples.english: The bark of this tree is rough.

bark.english: to make the short loud sound of a dog

beauty.english: the quality of being beautiful, or a beautiful person or thing

examples.english: Everyone admired the beauty of the lake.

bitter.english: having a sharp unpleasant taste, or full of anger and sadness

block.english: a solid piece of material, or an area surrounded by streets

examples.english: A fallen tree blocked the road.
examples.chinese: 一棵倒下的樹阻擋了道路。

bow.english: to bend your head or body forward as a greeting or sign of respect

examples.english: The actor bowed to the audience.

bow.english: a weapon for shooting arrows, or a knot with two loops

examples.english: She tied the ribbon in a bow.

brick.english: a hard block of baked clay used for building

examples.english: The wall is made of brick.
The bull stood in the field.

cap.english: a soft flat hat, or a cover for the top of a container

examples.english: Put the cap back on the bottle.

cap.english: to put an upper limit on something

chicken.english: a common farm bird, or its meat

close.english: to shut something or become shut
near in distance, time, or relationship

cloth.english: material made by weaving or knitting threads

examples.english: Wipe the table with a cloth.

coarse.english: rough in texture, or rude and offensive

collar.english: the part of a shirt around the neck, or a band around an animal’s neck

examples.english: The dog wore a red collar.

collection.english: a group of things gathered together, or the act of gathering them

examples.english: She has a collection of stamps.

come out.english: to appear, become available, or leave a place

examples.english: His new book will come out next month.

convey.english: to communicate an idea or transport something

cord.english: a long thin piece of string or covered wire

examples.english: Tie the box with a cord.

count.english: to say numbers in order or find the total number
the act of counting or the total number

couple.english: two people or things, or a small number

examples.english: The engine is coupled to the wheels.

cow.english: an adult female animal kept for milk or meat

examples.english: The cow gives milk every day.

crane.english: a tall machine for lifting heavy things, or a large long-legged bird

examples.english: The crane lifted the steel beam.

crew.english: the people who work on a ship, aircraft, or project

examples.chinese: 全體機組人員準備飛機。

current.english: a flow of water, air, or electricity

difference.english: the way in which two people or things are not the same

examples.english: There is a big difference between them.

drink.english: a liquid that you can drink

examples.english: I bought a cold drink after the run.
examples.chinese: 跑步後我買了一杯冷飲料。

drink.english: to take liquid into your mouth and swallow it

examples.english: Drink more water on hot days.
examples.chinese: 天氣熱的日子要多喝水。

drink.english: to drink alcohol, especially regularly or too much

examples.english: He does not drink before driving.

edge.english: the outside limit of a surface or area

examples.english: She sat on the edge of the bed.
Good design gives the product an edge.
examples.chinese: 良好的設計給產品帶來優勢。

edge.english: to move slowly and carefully in a particular direction

examples.english: The car edged forward in traffic.
examples.chinese: 汽車在車流中緩慢移動向前。

egg.english: a round object produced by a female bird, often eaten as food

examples.english: She boiled an egg for breakfast.

egg.english: to encourage someone to do something, often something unwise

examples.chinese: 他的朋友慫恿他繼續做。

fault.english: responsibility for a mistake or problem

examples.english: The accident was not your fault.
examples.chinese: 這次意外不是你的過失。

fault.english: a weakness or problem in something or someone

examples.english: The machine has a serious fault.

fault.english: to criticize someone or something for a mistake

figure.english: a number, especially in official information

examples.english: The sales figures were higher this year.

figure.english: a person, especially one who is important or well known

examples.english: She is a leading figure in science.
examples.chinese: 她是科學界的重要人物。

figure.english: the shape of a person or object

examples.english: A dark figure stood by the door.
examples.chinese: 一個黑色身形站在門旁。

figure.english: to think or calculate that something is true or likely

examples.english: I figure the trip will take two hours.
examples.chinese: 我估計這趟旅程要兩小時。

iron.english: a strong hard metal used for making tools, machines, and buildings

examples.english: The gate is made of iron.
examples.chinese: 這道閘門是用鐵製成的。

iron.english: a tool used for making clothes smooth with heat

examples.english: The iron is too hot for this shirt.
examples.chinese: 這個熨斗對這件襯衫來說太熱。

iron.english: to make clothes smooth using an iron

examples.chinese: 他們打開了鐵製的閘門。

level.english: the amount, standard, or degree of something

examples.english: Noise levels were high in the room.
examples.chinese: 房間內的噪音水平很高。

level.english: a floor or layer in a building or system

examples.english: The restaurant is on the top level.
Put the table on a level floor.
examples.chinese: 把桌子放在平坦的地板上。

level.english: to make something flat or equal in height

machine.english: a device with moving parts that uses power to do work
to make or shape something using a machine

examples.english: The parts are machined from steel.
examples.chinese: 這些零件是用機器加工鋼材製成的。

drum.english: a musical instrument that you hit with your hands or sticks
a large round container or cylinder

examples.english: Oil was stored in a metal drum.

drum.english: to play a drum or make a regular beating sound

drunk.english: unable to behave normally because of alcohol

examples.english: He was too drunk to walk home.
examples.chinese: 他醉酒得無法走路回家。

drunk.english: a person who is often or currently drunk

examples.english: A drunk shouted in the street.

dude.english: an informal word for a man or a person

examples.english: That dude helped me carry the box.

even.english: used to emphasize something surprising or extreme

examples.english: Even a child can understand this rule.
examples.chinese: 甚至小孩也能明白這條規則。

even.english: flat, level, or equal in amount

examples.english: Spread the paint in an even layer.
examples.chinese: 把油漆塗成均勻的一層。

exposure.english: the state of being affected by something or made known

examples.english: Long exposure to the sun can damage skin.
examples.chinese: 長時間暴露在陽光下會傷害皮膚。

fox.english: a wild animal like a dog with red-brown fur and a bushy tail

examples.english: A fox ran across the field.
The staff were friendly and helpful.

fry.english: to cook food in hot oil or fat

examples.english: Fry the fish for five minutes.

fry.english: a long thin piece of potato cooked in hot oil

examples.english: He ate a plate of fries.

full.english: containing as much as possible, or having eaten enough

examples.english: The glass is full of water.

generation.english: all the people born and living at about the same time

examples.english: This song is popular with a younger generation.
examples.chinese: 這首歌受年輕一代歡迎。

ghosting.english: the act of suddenly ending contact with someone without explanation

examples.chinese: 突然斷聯會令人很受傷。

go out.english: to leave home for entertainment or to have a romantic relationship

examples.english: They go out for dinner every Friday.
examples.chinese: 他們每個星期五外出吃晚餐。

golden.english: bright yellow like gold, or very valuable

graphic.english: relating to pictures, or described very clearly

examples.english: The report includes a graphic image.

gross.english: total before deductions, or very unpleasant

examples.english: The gross income was listed first.

gut.english: to remove the inside parts of an animal or destroy the inside of a building

examples.chinese: 大火徹底破壞了房屋內部。

guy.english: an informal word for a man or person

examples.english: The guy at the desk smiled.

herb.english: a plant used for flavoring food or as medicine

examples.chinese: 羅勒是我最喜歡的香草。

herd.english: a group of animals of the same kind

examples.english: A herd of cows crossed the road.

herd.english: to move animals or people together as a group

hurt.english: to cause pain or feel pain

examples.english: My knee hurts after the run.
She looked hurt by his words.
examples.chinese: 她看起來被他的話傷害了。

juice.english: liquid from fruit, vegetables, or meat

examples.english: Orange juice is on the table.

jump.english: to push yourself off the ground into the air

examples.english: The cat jumped over the wall.

kiss.english: to touch someone with your lips to show love or greeting

examples.english: She kissed the baby on the cheek.

kiss.english: an act of touching someone with the lips

examples.english: He gave her a kiss goodbye.
examples.chinese: 他給了她一個告別親吻。

layer.english: one thickness or sheet of material over or under another

lens.english: a curved piece of glass or plastic used in glasses or cameras

light.english: brightness that lets you see, or a lamp
not heavy, or pale in color
to start a fire or make a place bright

examples.chinese: 所有活著的生物都需要水。

examples.english: He earns a living by teaching.

lose.english: to no longer have something, or not win

low.english: not high in position, amount, or level
in or to a low position or level

examples.english: She spoke low so no one heard.
examples.chinese: 她低聲地說話，所以沒有人聽見。

mass.english: a large amount or large group of people or things

examples.english: A mass of people filled the square.
The city built a mass transport system.
examples.chinese: 城市建立了大規模運輸系統。

mix.english: to combine two or more things

examples.english: The car entered a narrow street.
examples.chinese: 汽車駛進一條狹窄的街道。

narrow.english: to become or make something less wide or less broad

network.english: a system of connected people, things, or computers
to meet people who may be useful professionally

examples.english: She likes to network at conferences.
examples.chinese: 她喜歡在會議上建立人脈。

oil.english: a thick liquid used as fuel, lubricant, or food

examples.english: Heat the oil in a pan.

paragraph.english: a section of writing that begins on a new line

perfect.english: having no faults or exactly right

examples.english: The weather was perfect for a walk.

perfect.english: to make something as good as possible

examples.english: She practiced to perfect her speech.

pick.english: to choose something or take flowers or fruit from a plant

examples.english: Pick a card from the deck.

pick.english: a choice or selected person or thing

examples.english: This restaurant is my top pick.
examples.chinese: 這間餐廳是我的首選選擇。

pop.english: to make a short sharp sound or appear suddenly

examples.english: She listens to pop every day.

race.english: a competition to see who is fastest
to compete in speed or move very quickly

rent.english: money paid regularly for using a home, room, or land
to pay to use something or let someone use it for money

examples.chinese: 我們租用了一間小公寓。

rice.english: small white or brown grains eaten as food

right.english: correct, suitable, or on the side opposite the left
something you are morally or legally allowed to do or have

examples.english: Everyone has the right to speak.

right.english: exactly or toward the right side

rob.english: to steal from a person or place by force or threat

route.english: the way from one place to another

examples.english: This is the fastest route home.

scratch.english: to rub something with nails or a sharp object

examples.english: There is a scratch on the table.

sheet.english: a large piece of cloth for a bed, or a piece of paper

examples.english: Write your name on this sheet.
examples.chinese: 把你的名字寫在這張紙上。

short.english: measuring little in length, height, or time

shoulder.english: the part of the body between the neck and arm

examples.english: She carried the bag on her shoulder.

shoulder.english: to accept responsibility or a burden

snow.english: soft white pieces of frozen water that fall from the sky
to have snow falling from the sky

solution.english: a way to solve a problem

examples.chinese: 我們需要更好的解決方法。

solution.english: a liquid mixture in which a substance is dissolved

examples.english: Salt solution can clean the wound.
examples.chinese: 鹽水溶液可以清潔傷口。

spell.english: to say or write the letters of a word in order
a short period of time, or words believed to have magic power

examples.english: There was a spell of dry weather.

spend.english: to use money, time, or energy

examples.english: Do not spend all your money.

square.english: a shape with four equal sides, or an open public area

examples.english: Draw a square on the paper.

square.english: shaped like a square or measuring an area unit

examples.english: The room has a square table.
examples.chinese: 房間裏有一張正方形的桌子。

stage.english: a part of a process, or a raised area for performance

examples.english: The project is in its final stage.
examples.chinese: 這個項目處於最後階段。

stage.english: to organize an event or perform a play

examples.chinese: 學校舉行了一場音樂會。

star.english: a bright object in the sky, or a famous performer

examples.english: A bright star shone above us.
examples.chinese: 一顆明亮的星星在我們上方閃耀。

star.english: to be the main performer in a film, play, or show

examples.english: She starred in a new film.
The lake was still at dawn.
He threw a small stone into the river.
examples.chinese: 他把一顆小石頭丟進河裏。

stream.english: a small river, or a continuous flow of data

examples.english: A clear stream ran through the valley.
examples.chinese: 一條清澈的小溪流過山谷。

stream.english: to play media over the internet or flow continuously

examples.chinese: 我們在線上串流播放那部電影。

string.english: thin cord, or a series of things

well.english: in a good or satisfactory way

examples.english: I hope you feel well soon.
examples.chinese: 我希望你很快恢復健康。

well.english: a deep hole in the ground for getting water

examples.english: The village has an old well.

wine.english: an alcoholic drink made from grapes

examples.english: They served red wine with dinner.
examples.chinese: 他們晚餐供應紅葡萄酒。
